(born April 23, 1971) is an American former television writer, producer and comic book writer. He is best known as the co-creator of the television series *[The Flash](/wiki/The_Flash_(2014_TV_series) "The Flash (2014 TV series)")*, *[Arrow](/wiki/Arrow_(TV_series) "Arrow (TV series)")*, *[Supergirl](/wiki/Supergirl_(TV_series) "Supergirl (TV series)")* and *[Legends of Tomorrow](/wiki/Legends_of_Tomorrow "Legends of Tomorrow")*.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American television writer and producer

| Andrew Kreisberg | |
| --- | --- |
| Kreisberg at the 2012 [San Diego Comic-Con](/wiki/San_Diego_Comic-Con "San Diego Comic-Con") | |
| Born | (1971-04-23) April 23, 1971 (age 54) |
| Education | * [Boston University](/wiki/Boston_University "Boston University") * [College of Communication](/wiki/Boston_University_College_of_Communication "Boston University College of Communication") * Class of 1993 |
| Occupations | Writer, producer |
| Years active | 1998–2017 |

**Andrew Kreisberg** (born April 23, 1971) is an American former television writer, producer and comic book writer. He is best known as the co-creator of the television series *[The Flash](/wiki/The_Flash_(2014_TV_series) "The Flash (2014 TV series)")*, *[Arrow](/wiki/Arrow_(TV_series) "Arrow (TV series)")*, *[Supergirl](/wiki/Supergirl_(TV_series) "Supergirl (TV series)")* and *[Legends of Tomorrow](/wiki/Legends_of_Tomorrow "Legends of Tomorrow")*.

## Personal life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrew_Kreisberg&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Personal life and education")]

Kreisberg graduated from the [Boston University](/wiki/Boston_University "Boston University") [College of Communication](/wiki/Boston_University_College_of_Communication "Boston University College of Communication") in 1993.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Kreisberg is [Jewish](/wiki/American_Jews "American Jews").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrew_Kreisberg&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### Television

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrew_Kreisberg&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Television")]

His first job was on the short-lived [animated sitcom](/wiki/Animated_sitcom "Animated sitcom") *[Mission Hill](/wiki/Mission_Hill_(TV_series) "Mission Hill (TV series)")*. He has written for several other series including: *[Justice League](/wiki/Justice_League_(TV_series) "Justice League (TV series)")*, *[The Simpsons](/wiki/The_Simpsons "The Simpsons")*, *[Hope & Faith](/wiki/Hope_%26_Faith "Hope & Faith")*, *[Boston Legal](/wiki/Boston_Legal "Boston Legal")*, *[Lipstick Jungle](/wiki/Lipstick_Jungle_(TV_series) "Lipstick Jungle (TV series)")*, *[Eli Stone](/wiki/Eli_Stone "Eli Stone")*, *[The Vampire Diaries](/wiki/The_Vampire_Diaries "The Vampire Diaries")*, *[Star Wars: The Clone Wars](/wiki/Star_Wars:_The_Clone_Wars_(2008_TV_series) "Star Wars: The Clone Wars (2008 TV series)")*, *[My Family](/wiki/My_Family "My Family")*, and *[Warehouse 13](/wiki/Warehouse_13 "Warehouse 13")*.

In November 2015, Kreisberg signed a multi-year deal with [Warner Bros. Television](/wiki/Warner_Bros._Television "Warner Bros. Television"), through which he would continue to develop new projects as well as remain the sole showrunner of *The Flash*, co-showrunner of *Supergirl* (along with [Ali Adler](/wiki/Ali_Adler "Ali Adler")), and executive producer on *Arrow* and *Legends of Tomorrow*.[[3]](#cite_note-THRdeal-3) In November 2017, his position with Warner Bros. was terminated due to allegations of [sexual harassment](/wiki/Sexual_harassment "Sexual harassment").[[4]](#cite_note-Fired-4)

#### *Fringe*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrew_Kreisberg&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Fringe")]

In 2009, he joined the [FOX](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Network "Fox Broadcasting Network") [science-fiction](/wiki/U.S._television_science_fiction "U.S. television science fiction")/[horror](/wiki/Horror_fiction "Horror fiction") series *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")* as a co-executive producer and writer. At the end of [season one](/wiki/Fringe_(season_1) "Fringe (season 1)"), Kreisberg left the show. He co-wrote the following episodes:

* "[Midnight](/wiki/Midnight_(Fringe) "Midnight (Fringe)")" (01.18) (co-written with co-executive producer [J.H. Wyman](/wiki/J.H._Wyman "J.H. Wyman"))
* "[Unearthed](/wiki/Unearthed_(Fringe) "Unearthed (Fringe)")" (02.11) (co-written with co-executive producer [David H. Goodman](/wiki/David_H._Goodman "David H. Goodman"))

#### *Booster Gold*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrew_Kreisberg&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Booster Gold")]

In 2011, Kreisberg was hired to write a pilot based on [DC Comics](/wiki/DC_Comics "DC Comics") hero [Booster Gold](/wiki/Booster_Gold "Booster Gold"), for [Syfy](/wiki/Syfy "Syfy").[[5]](#cite_note-HR1-5) The script went through many iterations and was said to be in consideration at the network.[[6]](#cite_note-Gold1-6)[[7]](#cite_note-Gold2-7) This project was shelved since Warner Bros. severed all ties with Kreisberg in November 2017.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

#### Arrowverse

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrew_Kreisberg&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Arrowverse")]

In 2011, Kreisberg, [Marc Guggenheim](/wiki/Marc_Guggenheim "Marc Guggenheim"), and [Greg Berlanti](/wiki/Greg_Berlanti "Greg Berlanti") began developing *[Arrow](/wiki/Arrow_(TV_series) "Arrow (TV series)")*, a re-imagining of the [DC Comics](/wiki/DC_Comics "DC Comics") comic book character [Green Arrow](/wiki/Green_Arrow "Green Arrow") for [The CW](/wiki/The_CW "The CW"). In January 2012, The CW picked the project up to [pilot](/wiki/Television_pilot "Television pilot").[[9]](#cite_note-Deadline1-9) On January 31, 2012, actor [Stephen Amell](/wiki/Stephen_Amell "Stephen Amell") was cast in the title role of Oliver Queen/Arrow.[[10]](#cite_note-Deadline2-10) On May 11, 2012, The CW order *Arrow* to series. It premiered on October 10, 2012.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

In July 2013, it was announced that Kreisberg, fellow *Arrow* co-creator Berlanti and DC Comics CCO [Geoff Johns](/wiki/Geoff_Johns "Geoff Johns") would be introducing [Barry Allen](/wiki/Barry_Allen "Barry Allen") during the show's second season, with the twentieth episode serving as a [backdoor pilot](/wiki/Television_pilot#Backdoor_pilot "Television pilot"). Actor [Grant Gustin](/wiki/Grant_Gustin "Grant Gustin") was cast and first appeared in the eighth episode of the second season, "The Scientist".[[12]](#cite_note-HR2-12) The CW producers were pleased with the handling of the character, and greenlit a pilot episode, foregoing the backdoor version.[[13]](#cite_note-Deadline3-13) In May 2014, *[The Flash](/wiki/The_Flash_(2014_TV_series) "The Flash (2014 TV series)")* was officially ordered to series.[[14]](#cite_note-Deadline4-14) It premiered October 7, 2014.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

On February 26, 2015, it was announced that Kreisberg, along with Guggenheim and Berlanti, would write/executive produce a spin-off series featuring [Caity Lotz](/wiki/Caity_Lotz "Caity Lotz"), [Victor Garber](/wiki/Victor_Garber "Victor Garber"), [Brandon Routh](/wiki/Brandon_Routh "Brandon Routh") and [Wentworth Miller](/wiki/Wentworth_Miller "Wentworth Miller") as [White Canary](/wiki/Sara_Lance "Sara Lance"), [Martin Stein](/wiki/Martin_Stein_(Arrowverse) "Martin Stein (Arrowverse)"), the [Atom](/wiki/Ray_Palmer_(Arrowverse) "Ray Palmer (Arrowverse)"), and [Captain Cold](/wiki/Leonard_Snart_(Arrowverse) "Leonard Snart (Arrowverse)") respectively, for a potential 2016 premiere.[[16]](#cite_note-Deadline6-16) The series, titled *[Legends of Tomorrow](/wiki/Legends_of_Tomorrow "Legends of Tomorrow")* premiered on January 21, 2016.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

### Comics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrew_Kreisberg&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Comics")]

Kreisberg has written for the comic book series *[Green Arrow and Black Canary](/wiki/Green_Arrow_and_Black_Canary "Green Arrow and Black Canary")* and *[Batman Confidential](/wiki/Batman_Confidential "Batman Confidential").*

In 2008, Arcana Comics began publishing *Helen Killer*, a comic book by Kreisberg with art by Matthew Rice. In it, a college-aged [Helen Keller](/wiki/Helen_Keller "Helen Keller") is given a device which allows her to see and hear and which increases her physical abilities, at which point she is hired to protect the President of the United States.

It was announced in July 2014, that Kreisberg and *Arrow* executive story editor [Ben Sokolowski](/wiki/Ben_Sokolowski "Ben Sokolowski") would be taking over the [Green Arrow](/wiki/Green_Arrow "Green Arrow") title in October of that year, beginning with issue #35.[[18]](#cite_note-IGN1-18)

## Allegations of sexual harassment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrew_Kreisberg&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Allegations of sexual harassment")]

On November 10, 2017, Kreisberg was suspended from his role as showrunner on *The Flash, Arrow*, *Legends of Tomorrow*, and *Supergirl*, after fifteen women and four men accused him of [sexual harassment](/wiki/Sexual_harassment "Sexual harassment").[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20) On November 29, 2017, he was fired from all Warner Bros. Television projects.[[4]](#cite_note-Fired-4)[[21]](#cite_note-21)

In October 2023, *[Vanity Fair](/wiki/Vanity_Fair_(magazine) "Vanity Fair (magazine)")* reported that Kreisberg had been arrested in March 2023 and charged with [forcible touching](/wiki/Forcible_touching "Forcible touching") following an alleged incident at a [bar mitzvah](/wiki/Bar_mitzvah "Bar mitzvah") in Westchester County, New York. The charges against Kreisberg were later dropped.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

## Filmography

## Bibliography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrew_Kreisberg&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Bibliography")]

### DC Comics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrew_Kreisberg&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: DC Comics")]

* *[JLA: Classified](/wiki/JLA:_Classified "JLA: Classified")* #49: "To Live in Hearts We Leave Behind" (with Paulo Siqueira, 2008)
* *[Batman Confidential](/wiki/Batman_Confidential "Batman Confidential")* #22–25, 29 & 30 (with [Scott McDaniel](/wiki/Scott_McDaniel "Scott McDaniel"), 2008–2009) collected as *Batman Confidential: Dead to Rights* (tpb, 144 pages, 2010, [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4012-2925-5](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4012-2925-5 "Special:BookSources/1-4012-2925-5"))
* [Green Arrow](/wiki/Green_Arrow "Green Arrow"):
  + *[Green Arrow and Black Canary](/wiki/Green_Arrow_and_Black_Canary "Green Arrow and Black Canary")* (with [Mike Norton](/wiki/Mike_Norton "Mike Norton") and Renato Guedes (co-features in #25–28), 2009–2010) collected as:
    - *Enemies List* (collects #15–20, tpb, 144 pages, 2009, [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-401-22498-9](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-401-22498-9 "Special:BookSources/1-401-22498-9"))
    - *Big Game* (collects #21–26, tpb, 192 pages, 2010, [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-401-22709-0](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-401-22709-0 "Special:BookSources/1-401-22709-0"))
    - *Five Stages* (collects #27–29, tpb, 128 pages, 2010, [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4012-2898-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4012-2898-4 "Special:BookSources/1-4012-2898-4"))
  + *[Arrow Volume 1](/wiki/Arrow_(TV_series) "Arrow (TV series)")* (tpb, 208 pages, 2013, [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4012-4299-5](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4012-4299-5 "Special:BookSources/1-4012-4299-5")) includes:
    - *Arrow: Test Drive* (co-written by Kreisberg, [Marc Guggenheim](/wiki/Marc_Guggenheim "Marc Guggenheim"), [Greg Berlanti](/wiki/Greg_Berlanti "Greg Berlanti") and [Geoff Johns](/wiki/Geoff_Johns "Geoff Johns"), art by Omar Francia, [digital](/wiki/Digital_comics "Digital comics") [one-shot](/wiki/One-shot_(comics) "One-shot (comics)"), 2012)
    - *Arrow* #1, 2 & 4 (co-written by Kreisberg and Marc Guggenheim, art by [Mike Grell](/wiki/Mike_Grell "Mike Grell") (#1–2) and Eric Nguyen (#4), digital, 2012)
  + *[Green Arrow](/wiki/The_New_52 "The New 52")* vol. 5 #35–40 (co-written by Kreisberg and [Ben Sokolowski](/wiki/Ben_Sokolowski "Ben Sokolowski"), art by Daniel Sampere, 2012) collected in *Green Arrow: Kingdom* (tpb, 144 pages, 2015, [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4012-5762-3](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4012-5762-3 "Special:BookSources/1-4012-5762-3"))
* *[Superman: World of New Krypton](/wiki/Superman:_World_of_New_Krypton "Superman: World of New Krypton")* (with [Pete Woods](/wiki/Pete_Woods "Pete Woods");[[23]](#cite_note-23) Kreisberg left the series before the release of the first issue and the series ended up being co-written by [Greg Rucka](/wiki/Greg_Rucka "Greg Rucka") and [James Robinson](/wiki/James_Robinson_(writer) "James Robinson (writer)"))[[24]](#cite_note-24)
* *[Justice League of America's Vibe](/wiki/Vibe_(character) "Vibe (character)")* #1 & 2 (co-written by Kreisberg and Geoff Johns, art by Pete Woods, 2013) collected in *Justice League of America's Vibe: Breach* (tpb, 232 pages, 2014, [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4012-4331-2](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4012-4331-2 "Special:BookSources/1-4012-4331-2"))

### Other publishers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrew_Kreisberg&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Other publishers")]

* *Hellen Killer* #1–4 (with Matthew Rice, [Arcana Studios](/wiki/Arcana_Studios "Arcana Studios"), 2007–2008)
* *[Darksiders II](/wiki/Darksiders_II "Darksiders II")* #1–5 (with Roger Robinson; issues #3–5 are co-written by Kreisberg and David Slagle, digital, [Dark Horse](/wiki/Dark_Horse_Comics "Dark Horse Comics"), 2012) collected as *Darksiders II: Death's Door* (hc, 64 pages, 2012, [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-61655-026-0](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-61655-026-0 "Special:BookSources/1-61655-026-0"))
